zotero.abstractNote: We study stochastic gradient descent-ascent (SGDA) for solving finite-sum minimax optimization problems. While standard SGDA typically relies on sampling with replacement, we show that sampling without replacement (shuffling) leads to faster convergence rates. Specifically, for strongly-convex-strongly-concave objectives, we prove that shuffling achieves a convergence rate of O(1/nˆ2 + 1/T) where n is the number of components and T is the number of iterations, improving upon the O(1/n + 1/T) rate of sampling with replacement. We provide a unified analysis for both smooth and non-smooth settings and demonstrate the effectiveness of our theoretical findings through numerical experiments.
